description_long: This object is a collection of various axes, each with distinct blade shapes and handle designs, arranged in a single row. The axes feature a mix of single and double-bladed heads, with some blades having ornate or fantasy-inspired curves and cutouts. The handles vary in length and curvature, with some being straight and others featuring ergonomic bends or decorative flares. The materials appear to be primarily metal for the blades and wood or metal for the handles, with some axes showing signs of wear, patina, or colored accents. The blades range from traditional crescent shapes to more elaborate, fantasy-style forms, and some axes have reinforced or capped ends. This set would be suitable for display, collection, or use in themed urban environments such as workshops, garages, or as props.
description: A diverse set of axes with various blade shapes and handle designs, featuring both traditional and fantasy-inspired elements. The collection includes single and double-bladed axes with metal heads and wood or metal handles.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the full array of axes, highlighting the diversity in blade shapes and handle styles.
description_view_1: A side view emphasizing the thickness of the blades and the length of the handles, with blade profiles clearly visible.
description_view_2: A rear view displaying the backs of the axe heads and the curvature of the handles, with less detail on the blade faces.
description_view_3: An opposite side view to view 1, again showing blade thickness and handle alignment, with some decorative features visible.
attribute_handle_type: Handles are mostly straight or slightly curved, made of wood or metal, with some featuring ergonomic grips or decorative flares; cross-sections are generally oval or round.
attribute_blade_design: Blades include single and double-edged designs, with some featuring ornate curves, cutouts, and fantasy-inspired shapes; edges are sharp with some axes having beards or reinforced polls.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and pinned mounting styles are visible, with some axes possibly using modern composite or decorative mounts.
